what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment record that links a design-source channel entry to an image asset located at image://design-source/landing-mockups/p-3.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter with no body content.
The target field uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/landing-mockups/p-3.
It records an added timestamp of 2026-06-22T11:31:00.585306+00:00.
standouts: The target uses an image:// URI scheme.
It belongs to the design-source channel.
It is a typed attachment metadata record with no explanatory body.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Attachment id uses an att- prefix.
References an external asset via a URI rather than embedding it.
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset.
concerns: The image:// URI cannot be resolved or validated from the contents of this file alone.
notes: A bare attachment metadata stub that records a design-source image reference to landing-mockups/p-3.
questions: What entity or message in the design-source channel is this attachment attached to?

purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an image attachment record, and the content matches that purpose by providing id, channel, type, target, and timestamp.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to catalog a design mockup image as a timestamped, typed attachment within a channel memory ledger.
novelty: The image:// URI scheme is a notable, non-filesystem way of addressing the asset.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, typed link between a channel entry and a specific image asset, supporting asset provenance and retrieval in a fused system.
